summary_one_line: A 4-byte little-endian prefix at offset 0 that differs from the WASM magic 0x6d736100 ('\0asm'), taking the magic-mismatch true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The first 4 bytes (read as uint32_t at offset 0) must NOT equal the WASM magic constant 0x6d736100 ('\0asm'), so the comparison `magic != wasm_magic` evaluates true and ReadMagic returns false.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry non-magic 4-byte prefixes (e.g. 7f 45 4c 4c, 4c 46 02 04, 7f 7f 7f ff, 01 01 00 00) so the uint32 magic differs from 0x6d736100 and the true branch is taken; the source overlay marks the return-false lines [W].
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed begins with the exact magic bytes 00 61 73 6d, which equals wasm_magic, so magic != wasm_magic is false and execution falls through into the version/section parsing path marked [L].
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts the integer CMP at line 77 and writes an operand-distance bucket into CMP_MAP; an input whose 4-byte prefix is farther from the magic constant produces a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, giving value_profile a gradient toward the magic-mismatch (true) side that naive's plain edge feedback lacks.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the integer comparison `magic != wasm_magic` and its true branch is the globally blocked side.
The constant compared against is the WASM magic 0x6d736100.
The true (mismatch) branch returning false is hit only by winner-resolving seeds.
Winner-resolving seeds carry non-magic 4-byte prefixes such as 7f 45 4c 4c.
Loser-blocking seeds begin with the exact WASM magic bytes 00 61 73 6d.
The byte diff confirms loser inputs are concentrated on the magic ASCII 'asm' bytes at offsets 1-3 while winner bytes differ.
value_profile rewards CMP operand pairs that move closer to / farther from matching via a new CMP_MAP bucket, which naive's plain edge feedback cannot supply.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: Neither involved fuzzer uses I2S substitution, ruling out a substitution mechanism.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is 'CMP_MAP gradient' (value_profile), not I2SRandReplace, so a lineage dash-row query is not mandatory; the MECHANISM CONTEXT section confirms value_profile uses CMP_MAP operand-distance buckets and explicitly has 'No `I2SRandReplace`', so no I2S floor signal would be expected.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a 4-byte uint32 == magic-constant equality in which value_profile shows no advantage over naive at reaching the mismatch (true) side, i.e. equal resolve rates across the sweep.

weakest_evidence_point: The CMP_MAP gradient claim assumes the magic mismatch is the value_profile-rewarded edge, but the true side is trivially reachable by almost any random prefix, so naive's loss may stem from corpus seeding rather than an active CMP_MAP gradient.
